opp.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Important' and Merlin plus Merlin Intake are attached, but the agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all empty. There is no rep-captured buyer rationale behind the 'Important' tag.
• The October 21 demo (63 minutes) with Erica Doucet-Maye, Leslie Crow, Scarlett Naquin and other BakerRipley stakeholders produced action items centered on Zycus-versus-Coupa differentiator decks and P2P invoicing, integrations and reporting deep dives. Merlin, intake-AI and agentic capabilities did not surface from the buyer.
• The March 17 follow-up was unrecorded but included Coupa, Conga and Ivalua reps, a competitive bake-off on classical P2P/SIM criteria. With Coupa displacement as the dominant theme and no buyer-voiced AI signals, this lands at Curious.

sources: Avoma demo call 21 Oct 2025: Melisa Miles (procurement) on ad-hoc sourcing pain
Avoma demo call 21 Oct 2025: Matthew Smith (contracts) on manual compliance workflows
Avoma demo call 21 Oct 2025: AP team on invoice mailbox delays
Avoma demo call 21 Oct 2025: legal/contracts on renewal tracking risk
metrics.narrative: Baker Ripley articulated specific operational pain during the 21 Oct 2025 demo: manual email-based sourcing without centralized RFI/RFP tooling (Melisa Miles, procurement lead), manual vendor compliance processing for W-9/TIN/SAM validations lacking automation (Matthew Smith, contracts), manual contract renewal tracking risking auto-renewals without utilization insight (legal/contracts team), and manual AP invoice mailbox classification leading to exceptions and delays (AP team). However, no quantified financial impact was stated — no cycle-time reduction targets, no FTE savings goals, no error-rate baselines, and no ROI requirement articulated on the call. The business case remains qualitative. With the RFP cancelled 20 May 2026 and the buyer silent for 60 days, the value case has not been reconfirmed or quantified, creating a coverage gap that blocks forecast defensibility.

sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Melisa Miles (procurement lead) articulated pain, no advocacy evidence
Avoma action item 21 Oct 2025: Erica Doucet-Maye to coordinate follow-up (never executed)
SF LastActivityDate 27 Apr 2026: tasks referencing Erica Doucet-Maye and Melisa Miles
SF Next Step 20 May 2026: RFP cancelled by 'BR Team', no champion intervention visible
champion.narrative: No strong or confirmed champion. Erica Doucet-Maye (Procurement Specialist) and Melisa Miles (Senior Manager, Purchasing and Vendor Relations) are the most engaged buyer contacts — both appear in Salesforce LastActivityDate tasks (27 Apr 2026) and were active in the 21 Oct 2025 demo. Melisa Miles holds the senior procurement role and articulated sourcing pain on the call, positioning her as the likely process owner, but she has shown no advocacy behavior: no introductions to the EB or legal leadership, no competitive intel shared, no internal championing of Zycus post-demo. Erica Doucet-Maye coordinated the demo and was named in the follow-up action item to schedule the next session, but that follow-up never materialized, and she has been silent since 27 Apr (60 days). Neither has pushed back on the RFP cancellation or re-engaged to discuss the redrawn scope. With the deal cold and no champion driving it internally, the relationship is passive at best. Building or identifying a champion with EB/DM access is a top-priority move if this deal is to be salvaged.

sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: buyer stated they recently demoed Coupa
Avoma action item 21 Oct 2025: send Coupa differentiator decks
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Zycus positioned homegrown CLM, Merlin AI, single codebase vs Coupa
Avoma key takeaway 21 Oct 2025: positive buyer reaction to Zycus differentiators
competition.narrative: Coupa is the confirmed and active competitor. Baker Ripley demoed Coupa before engaging Zycus, and during the 21 Oct 2025 Zycus demo the buyer explicitly requested in-demo Coupa comparisons and asked for Zycus-vs-Coupa differentiator decks to be sent post-call (action item, speaker_id 5). The Zycus team positioned against Coupa on three fronts during the demo: (1) homegrown, organically-developed CLM versus Coupa's acquired/bolt-on solutions, (2) deeper Merlin GenAI capabilities in CLM versus Coupa's AI, and (3) single-codebase architecture avoiding 'Frankenstein' integrations. The buyer's reaction to these differentiators was positive (key takeaway notes), suggesting Zycus gained ground on product fit, but no explicit competitive elimination or down-select statement has been made. With the RFP cancelled 20 May and the buyer silent for 60 days, Coupa's current status is unknown — they may have also been sidelined by the scope change, or they may be better-positioned if they have maintained contact. The competitive wedge is product architecture and AI depth, but without an active champion or recent engagement we have no line of sight into whether that wedge is holding. Re-engaging to understand the new scope and Coupa's current position is critical.

sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Melisa Miles on manual email sourcing
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Matthew Smith on manual compliance workflows
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: contracts team on renewal tracking risk
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: AP team (Neatheas Malveaux) on invoice mailbox delays
identify_pain.narrative: Baker Ripley's pain is well-documented from the 21 Oct 2025 demo and is operationally specific. Procurement lead Melisa Miles stated they execute sourcing via email and ad-hoc meetings with no centralized RFI/RFP tool, creating inefficiency and lack of auditability. Contracts lead Matthew Smith and legal (Leslie Crow) described manual vendor compliance processing — W-9s, TINs, SAM/TAG checks — without automated tax or banking validations, leading to downstream AP exceptions. The contracts team emphasized manual contract renewal tracking, risking auto-renewals without utilization insight or on-time renewal management. AP supervisor Neatheas Malveaux and the AP team highlighted that invoice submissions arrive in an email mailbox and must be manually sorted, classified, and matched, causing exceptions and process delays. The pain is concentrated in procurement, contracts, and AP, with legal/compliance as a secondary stakeholder. The pain owner is distributed across these three functions, with no single executive sponsor named. The pain remains valid but unquantified and has not been reconfirmed since the RFP cancellation 20 May.

sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: legal/contracts attendees present but no process discussion
Avoma action item 21 Oct 2025: plan follow-up on ERP integration (never executed)
No InfoSec, MSA, SOW, or legal-review timeline in any source
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, and procurement channel are entirely unmapped. The 21 Oct 2025 demo included Matthew Smith (Contracts Attorney Manager), Madonna Long (Contracts Manager), and Leslie Crow (Assistant General Counsel), signaling that legal/contracts will be involved, but no discussion of MSA, SOW, redline cycle, InfoSec review, or ERP integration scoping occurred on that call. The demo action items flagged a future deep-dive into ERP/third-party integrations and P2P invoice automation, but that follow-up never happened. With the RFP cancelled 20 May and the deal cold, the paper process is a black box. We do not know whether BakerRipley requires InfoSec/CISO sign-off, whether implementation will be Zycus-led or require an SI partner, or what the contract approval chain looks like. Mapping the paper process is essential if the deal is revived, because legal/InfoSec can add 30-60 days to close in enterprise Source-to-Pay deals.

sources: Avoma demo attendee list 21 Oct 2025: no C-level or confirmed budget authority present
SF OpportunityContactRole: no Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role flagged
SF Next Step 20 May 2026: RFP cancelled by 'BR Team', no named decision authority
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified across any source. The 21 Oct 2025 demo included procurement (Melisa Miles, Senior Manager Purchasing), contracts (Matthew Smith, Madonna Long), legal (Leslie Crow, Assistant General Counsel), finance/AP (Neatheas Malveaux, AP Supervisor; Ha Vu, Senior Accounting Manager; Shelbie Boltin, Sr Accounting Manager), and the controller Muhammadou Drammeh (Sr Director), but no discussion of budget authority, no pricing conversation, and no CFO or C-level sponsor participation. The Salesforce contact roster lists Muhammadou Drammeh (Controller/Sr Director) and Shawn Jackson (Senior Director), either of whom could hold budget authority, but neither has been confirmed in that role on a call or via a Contact Role flag. With the deal cold for 60 days and the RFP cancelled, the budget holder is unmapped, which is a critical gap for a $150k enterprise Source-to-Pay deal requiring multi-year investment approval. Mapping the EB is the highest-priority requalification step if this deal is to be revived.

sources: SF Next Step history Aug 2025-May 2026: RFQ/RFP timeline
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: cross-functional committee present
SF Next Step 20 May 2026: RFP cancelled, scope being changed
No stated approval chain or governance model in any source
decision_process.narrative: The decision process inferred from the call sequence and Next Step history: (1) initial discovery and RFQ phase Aug-Oct 2025, (2) demo stage 21 Oct 2025 with cross-functional buying committee (procurement, contracts, legal, finance/AP), (3) RFP issuance planned for Nov 2025-Jan 2026, (4) RFP received 26 Aug 2025, submitted 12 Sep 2025, awaiting vendor shortlist Oct 2025-Feb 2026, (5) formal RFP reissued and received 20 Apr 2026, (6) RFP extended to 12 May 2026, then (7) RFP cancelled 20 May 2026 with scope being redrawn. The process stalled at RFP submission/evaluation before reaching shortlist, commercials, or legal. No approval chain has been articulated — we do not know whether CFO, board, or executive committee sign-off is required, nor whether this is a sole-sourced or competitive final selection. The buying committee is cross-functional but no single process owner or project lead has been named. The RFP cancellation suggests either scope creep, budget reallocation, or internal misalignment, but the root cause is unknown because the buyer has been silent for 60 days.

sources: Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Teams intake adoption discussion
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: questions on autonomous negotiation AI, contract OCR, AP automation
Avoma demo 21 Oct 2025: Matthew Smith question on sourcing-to-contract rollover and ERP tie-in

Avoma action item 21 Oct 2025: send Coupa differentiator decks
decision_criteria.narrative: Baker Ripley's evaluation criteria were surfaced during the 21 Oct 2025 demo and are anchored to their pain points and feature questions. Key criteria include: (1) ease of adoption and change management, validated through interest in Microsoft Teams-embedded intake to meet users where they work (emphasized by procurement team); (2) AI capabilities for automation, explored through questions on autonomous negotiation for tail-spend, AI contract discovery/OCR, and AP invoice auto-classification; (3) integration architecture, specifically whether sourcing awards can roll into contracts and whether the platform integrates with their ERP (Matthew Smith, contracts); (4) homegrown vs acquired/bolt-on architecture, with strong positive reaction to Zycus single-codebase story versus 'Frankenstein' competitors; (5) Coupa feature parity and differentiation, as they requested in-demo comparison decks post-call. The criteria are confirmed but not formalized — no scoring matrix, no weighted RFP rubric, and no written BRD has been shared. With the RFP cancelled 20 May, the criteria may be shifting as scope is redrawn.

champion_strength.summary: No strong champion. Melisa Miles (Senior Manager, Purchasing) is the most senior procurement contact and articulated pain on the 21 Oct 2025 demo, but she has shown no advocacy (no EB intros, no competitive intel, no internal push) and has been silent for 60 days post-RFP cancellation. Erica Doucet-Maye (Procurement Specialist) coordinated the demo but has also been passive. Neither has driven the deal forward or re-engaged after the RFP was cancelled 20 May. Without a champion, Zycus has no control, no visibility into the redrawn scope or Coupa's position, and no line into the EB/DM layer.

Planned follow-up session from Oct 2025 never executed. Buyer has not re-engaged to discuss redrawn scope. No champion pushing internally. north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 111 (15 Oct 2026). Created 26 Aug 2025; 304 days old. Stage Formal Evaluation since creation. Last Salesforce activity 27 Apr 2026 (60 days ago). Next Step last updated 20 May 2026 (37 days ago) stating RFP cancelled and scope being redrawn. Zero buyer engagement since then. No call activity since 21 Oct 2025 demo (8+ months). Time required to complete RFP reissue, submission, evaluation, shortlist, commercials, negotiation, legal and close is minimum 120-150 days under aggressive enterprise timelines; current 111 days insufficient even if buyer re-engaged today. Deal is cold and date indefensible.

north_star_verdict.headline: RFP cancelled 20 May, buyer completely dark for 60 days with zero response to outreach. No call activity in 8 months. Deal is cold with no active champion or process, requiring immediate executive intervention to resurrect.

flags: Identify or build a champion with EB/DM access — Melisa Miles (procurement lead) and Erica Doucet-Maye are engaged contacts but have shown no advocacy; without a champion driving the redrawn RFP internally, Zycus has no control and no visibility into competitive position.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is cold (60 days since last activity), RFP cancelled 37 days ago, and close date 111 days out is now indefensible. Immediate re-engagement required to understand new scope, requalify buyer commitment, and map EB/champion. If buyer is unresponsive or scope is vague, recommend disqualify and reallocate capacity.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the sole identified competitor and remains the primary threat. Baker Ripley demoed Coupa before Zycus (pre-Oct 2025) and requested Coupa differentiator materials during the Zycus demo 21 Oct 2025. Zycus positioned successfully on homegrown CLM, Merlin AI depth, and single-codebase architecture versus Coupa's acquired/bolt-on stack, and the buyer's reaction was positive. However, with the RFP cancelled 20 May 2026 and the buyer silent for 60 days, Coupa's current position is unknown. If Coupa has maintained contact through the RFP cancellation and scope redraw, they may have gained ground; if they also went dark, the competitive field may have reset. Threat level is medium (not high) because the RFP process that would have forced a bake-off was aborted, and the deal is currently frozen rather than actively contested. The wedge is product architecture and AI — Zycus must reactivate it when the buyer re-engages.

However, fit is unconfirmed against the new scope being drawn post-RFP cancellation 20 May.

value.how_we_win: Homegrown CLM and Merlin AI depth versus Coupa's acquired bolt-ons; single-codebase simplicity versus Frankenstein integrations; emphasize procurement-AP-contracts unification and Teams-embedded intake for adoption. Reactivate this wedge in the first re-engagement call and tie it to the redrawn scope.
